description:
You are a log-analyst who has just received three uncompressed web-server access logs that are already present on the machine.  
    Your goal is to archive them, extract them to a separate working area, and then create a very small summary that counts how
    many HTTP “404” responses each log contains.

    Existing material
    -----------------
    1. Directory with raw logs:  
       /home/user/logs/raw
    2. Files already inside it:  
       • access-2023-06-01.log  
       • access-2023-06-02.log  
       • access-2023-06-03.log  

    Objective
    ---------
    Perform all the work from the command-line (≈5-10 commands are expected, but there is no hard limit) so that when you are finished:

    1. A compressed archive called
          /home/user/archives/web-logs-2023.tar.gz
       exists and *only* contains the three log files, stored with the internal
       paths exactly as shown below (and in exactly this order when listed with
       “tar -tzf”):

          logs/raw/access-2023-06-01.log
          logs/raw/access-2023-06-02.log
          logs/raw/access-2023-06-03.log

       (No leading “./”, no extra directories, no other files.)

    2. All three log files have been extracted from that archive into the
       directory
          /home/user/analysis/weblogs/
       so that the following files exist and are readable *directly* in that
       directory (i.e. not inside a nested “logs/raw” sub-folder):

          /home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-01.log
          /home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-02.log
          /home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-03.log

    3. A report file called
          /home/user/analysis/weblogs/404_report.txt
       exists with *exactly* the four-line, newline-terminated text shown
       below (each “: ” is a literal colon followed by a single space):

          access-2023-06-01.log: 1
          access-2023-06-02.log: 1
          access-2023-06-03.log: 1
          TOTAL: 3

       • The per-file numbers are the counts of lines whose status code field
         is “404”.  
       • The TOTAL line is the sum of those three counts.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline character after the
         word “3”.

    4. The directory /home/user/analysis/weblogs/ only contains the three
       extracted *.log* files and the single 404_report.txt file—nothing else.

    Notes & constraints
    -------------------
    • Create any intermediate directories you need; do **not** use sudo.  
    • Use standard GNU/Linux command-line tools (mkdir, tar, gzip, grep, awk,
      etc.).  
    • Make sure paths and file names match the requirements *exactly* so that
      automated tests can verify your work.  
    • Feel free to display intermediary command output to the console; only the
      final file-system state is graded.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the autograder.
        These paths, files and exact contents exist before the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <directory path="/home/user/logs/raw" perms="rwxr-xr-x"/>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/raw/access-2023-06-01.log" perms="rw-r--r--"><![CDATA[
127.0.0.1 - - [01/Jun/2023:12:00:01 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.10 - - [01/Jun/2023:12:00:05 +0000] "GET /about.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/raw/access-2023-06-02.log" perms="rw-r--r--"><![CDATA[
127.0.0.1 - - [02/Jun/2023:13:15:45 +0000] "GET /contact.html HTTP/1.1" 404 256
192.168.1.11 - - [02/Jun/2023:13:16:01 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/raw/access-2023-06-03.log" perms="rw-r--r--"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.5 - - [03/Jun/2023:09:05:12 +0000] "GET /pricing.html HTTP/1.1" 404 128
10.0.0.6 - - [03/Jun/2023:09:07:33 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <expected_final_state>
        <file path="/home/user/archives/web-logs-2023.tar.gz" must_exist="yes"/>
        <archive_listing path="/home/user/archives/web-logs-2023.tar.gz"><![CDATA[
logs/raw/access-2023-06-01.log
logs/raw/access-2023-06-02.log
logs/raw/access-2023-06-03.log
]]></archive_listing>

        <directory path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs" must_exist="yes"/>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-01.log" must_exist="yes"/>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-02.log" must_exist="yes"/>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs/access-2023-06-03.log" must_exist="yes"/>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs/404_report.txt" must_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
access-2023-06-01.log: 1
access-2023-06-02.log: 1
access-2023-06-03.log: 1
TOTAL: 3
]]></file>

        <!-- ensure no stray files -->
        <dir_exact_contents path="/home/user/analysis/weblogs">
            access-2023-06-01.log
            access-2023-06-02.log
            access-2023-06-03.log
            404_report.txt
        </dir_exact_contents>
    </expected_final_state>
